join_order.note: customer bitset is probe-side lookup; orders is scanned sequentially with zone map
bloom filter on o_orderkeys applied before hash table probe to skip ~90% non-matching lineitem rows

filter_pushdown.note: Resolve 'BUILDING' to dictionary code at startup, compare int32 during scan
Zone map prunes ~55% of the 150 blocks (sorted table); remaining blocks still row-filtered
Zone map skips earliest ~45% of the 600 blocks (sorted table); first qualifying block is partial

customer_filter.rationale: c_custkey is a dense integer in [1, 1500000]; flat boolean array gives O(1) lookup with zero hash overhead; 1.5 MB fits comfortably in L3 cache (44 MB)

orders_bloom_filter.rationale: Built over ~1.35M o_orderkeys; 10 bits/element → ~1% FP rate; 212 KB fits in L2/L3; eliminates ~90% of cache-miss hash probes on 33M lineitem rows

orders_join_ht.rationale: 1.35M entries × (4+4+4 bytes/slot) at 75% load → ~2M slots; 32 MB fits in L3 (44 MB); open addressing avoids pointer chasing

aggregation.key: struct { int32_t l_orderkey; int32_t o_orderdate; int32_t o_shippriority; }
aggregation.rationale: ~1.35M groups → high cardinality; 16 partitions by hash(l_orderkey) → each thread owns a partition slice; no cross-thread contention during accumulation

topk_heap.rationale: K=10 << N → heap approach O(N log 10) ≈ O(N); each thread maintains local top-10, then 64 local heaps merged for global top-10

phases.note: Each c_custkey value is unique per row → writes to distinct positions in flat_array; no false sharing if stride > cache line
Zone-map-pruned parallel scan of orders blocks with customer bitset filter; collect qualifying rows thread-locally; then single-threaded HT insert (1.35M inserts, ~10 ms)
Zone-map-pruned parallel scan; bloom check before HT probe; thread-local aggregation maps per partition (key partitioned by hash(l_orderkey) & 0xF); no cross-thread writes
Each of 16 partitions merged independently; then parallel thread-local top-10 heaps; final merge of 64 local heaps in O(640 log 10)

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=150] then per block: [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size]
index_usage.fast_path_condition: block.max < 9204 → accept entire block without row-level check

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then per block: [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size]
index_usage.fast_path_condition: block.min > 9204 → accept entire block without row-level check

memory_notes.customer_bitset: 1.5 MB — permanently resident in L3 during orders and lineitem scans
memory_notes.orders_ht: ~32 MB — fits in L3 (44 MB); bloom filter (212 KB) provides L2-resident pre-check to avoid cold misses
memory_notes.lineitem_scan: streaming sequential reads (zone-map-pruned); random access only on bloom/HT probe for ~10% matching rows
memory_notes.revenue_computation: integer arithmetic only: l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) in scaled int64 (scale_factor=100^2=10000); avoids floating-point

revenue_formula.scale: result is in units of 10000 (scale_factor 100 × 100); divide by 10000 for final output
revenue_formula.type: int64_t accumulator per group (no overflow risk for SF10)

estimated_cost.target_vs_duckdb: DuckDB=81ms; target <150ms with zone-map pruning + bloom filter + 64-thread parallelism
estimated_cost.bottleneck: lineitem zone-map-pruned scan (330 blocks × 100K rows × 4 cols) + orders HT probe with bloom pre-filter
